title: Proclamation No. 165, s. 1967
text:
MALACAÑANG RESIDENCE OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 165

ESTABLISHING AS SAN MIGUEL FOREST RESERVE FOR WOOD PRODUCTION, WATERSHED, SOIL PROTECTION AND OTHER FOREST USES, THE PARCEL OF PUBLIC DOMAIN KNOWN AS TIMBERLAND, BULACAN LAND CLASSIFICATION PROJECT NO. 1-F, PER B.F. MAP L . C . -2178, SITUATED IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF SAN MIGUEL, PROVINCE OF BULACAN, PHILIPPINES.

Upon recommendation of the Director of Forestry, approved by the Secretary of Agriculture and Natural Resources and pursuant to the provisions of Section 1826 of the Revised Administrative Code, as amended by Republic Act No. 3092, I, FERDINAND E. MARCOS, President of the Philippines, do hereby withdraw from entry, sale, settlement or other disposition and establish as SAN MIGUEL FOREST RESERVE, subject to private rights if any there be, the parcel of public domain known as Timber Land, Bulacan Land Classification Project No. 1-F, situated in the Municipality of San Miguel, Province of Bulacan, Philippines, demarcated and established under Forestry Administrative Order No. 4-696, dated February 27, 1958, for wood production, watershed, soil protection and other forest purposes and place the same under the administration and control of the Director of Forestry who shall have the authority to regulate and use and occupancy of said Forest Reserve and the cutting, collecting and removing of timber and other forest products therein in accordance with forest laws and regulations.

The aforementioned parcel of public domain is shown and more particularly described in the attached Bureau of Forestry Map L . C . -2178, comprising the Timber Land of the aforementioned Bulacan Land Classification Project No. 1-F, containing an area of SIX THOUSAND FOUR HUNDRED EIGHTEEN (6,418) HECTARES, more or less.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 13th day of February, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and sixty-seven.

(SGD.) FERDINAND E. MARCOS President of the Philippines

By the President:

(SGD.) RAFAEL M. SALAS Executive Secretary

Source: CDAsia
